responseText:
**Rivian ( VIN 7PDSGABA4PN010679 – “ToughNut” ) – 27 Feb → 8 Mar 2026**

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| **Total distance driven** | **≈ 1 078 mi** (74 trips) |
| **Average efficiency** | **≈ 64 %** (real‑world range) |
| **Total charging energy** | **≈ 670 kWh** (17 sessions) |
| **Total charging cost** | **≈ $51 USD** (only a few sessions reported a cost) |
| **Days covered** | 10 calendar days (every day has at least one drive or charge) |

---

### Highlights – Notable Drives
| Date | From → To | Distance (mi) | Battery start → end | Efficiency % |
|------|-----------|---------------|---------------------|--------------|
| **1 Mar** | Ludlow, VT → Canaan, CT | **123.5** | 75 % → 17 % | 65 |
| **4 Mar** | Lebanon, NH → Holyoke, MA | **114.5** | 71 % → 20 % | 67 |
| **27 Feb** | Cornwall, CT → Bennington, VT | **93.6** | 67 % → 28 % | 73 |
| **3 Mar** | West Lebanon, NH → Stowe, VT | **77.2** | 74 % → 38 % | 64 |
| **2 Mar** | Great Barrington, MA → Dover, VT | **71.9** | 58 % → 23 % | 62 |

*These four trips account for **≈ 387 mi** (≈ 36 % of the total distance) and show the vehicle’s longest outings, each consuming a large chunk of the battery (down to 17‑23 % state‑of‑charge).*

### Highlights – Notable Charges
| Date | Location | Energy added (kWh) | Max power (kW) | DC‑fast? | Cost (USD) |
|------|----------|-------------------|----------------|----------|------------|
| **1 Mar** | Cornwall, CT (home) | **83.3** | 11 | No | **8.62** |
| **4 Mar** | Holyoke, MA (Supercharger) | **78.6** | 215 | Yes | 0 |
| **2 Mar** | Dover, VT (home) | **77.9** | 7 | No | 0 |
| **4 Mar** | Lebanon, NH (Supercharger) | **63.2** | 218 | Yes | 0 |
| **3 Mar** | West Lebanon, NH (Supercharger) | **58.1** | 217 | Yes | 0 |

*The biggest single charge (83 kWh) restored the battery from 19 % to 85 % and added ~224 mi of range – essentially a “full‑tank” fill‑up. The two fast‑charge sessions on 4 Mar delivered 78 kWh and 63 kWh in under 30 min each.*

### Day‑by‑Day Narrative

- **Feb 27** – A morning drive (≈ 94 mi) from Cornwall, CT to Bennington, VT took the battery down to ~28 %. A quick 27 kWh fast‑charge at Bennington topped it back up to 46 % for the evening.
- **Feb 28** – A short 14 kWh charge at Ludlow, VT (home) added ~140 mi of range, preparing for the next day’s long trip.
- **Mar 1** – The longest outing of the period: 123 mi from Ludlow to Canaan, draining the battery to 17 %. A night‑time home charge (83 kWh) restored the pack to 85 % and added 224 mi of range.
- **Mar 2** – A 72‑mi drive from Great Barrington to Dover reduced the battery to 23 %. An overnight home charge (78 kWh) at Dover lifted it back to 85 %.
- **Mar 3** – Two moderate trips (77 mi and 53 mi) between West Lebanon, Stowe, and Dummerston kept the battery in the 30‑70 % window. A brief 12 kWh top‑up at Dover added a little extra range.
- **Mar 4** – A 115‑mi drive from Lebanon to Holyoke left the battery at 20 %. A fast‑charge at Holyoke (78 kWh, 215 kW) quickly restored it to 75 % and added ~183 mi of range. Later, a 55‑mi drive to Montpelier was followed by a 45 kWh fast‑charge (215 kW) at Montpelier.
- **Mar 5** – Short local trips (≈ 35 mi each) between Windsor Locks, Torrington, and Cornwall kept the battery comfortably above 40 %. A 39 kWh home charge in Cornwall added ~98 mi of range.
- **Mar 6** – A 17‑mi round‑trip between Torrington and Cornwall was followed by a 14‑mi drive from Cornwall to Litchfield, both with modest battery usage. A tiny 2.5 kWh top‑up at Cornwall added ~2 mi of range.
- **Mar 7** – A 14‑mi charge at Cornwall (10 kW) added ~37 mi of range, keeping the vehicle ready for the final day.
- **Mar 8** – Two short trips (20 mi to Litchfield and 15 mi back to Cornwall) wrapped up the period, ending the day with the battery at ~18 % after a modest 15 kWh charge earlier in the day.

### Overall Take‑aways

- **Driving:** 1 078 mi in 10 days, averaging ~108 mi per day. The longest single drive (123 mi) and the highest‑efficiency short‑trip (19 mi at 96 % efficiency) illustrate both range‑demanding and efficient usage.
- **Charging:** 670 kWh of electricity consumed, roughly **$0.08 USD/kWh** on average (total cost ≈ $51). Home charging supplied the bulk of energy, while fast‑charging at Superchargers provided rapid top‑ups for longer trips.
- **Battery health:** No degradation data was requested, but the vehicle consistently delivered > 200 mi of range after a full charge, indicating healthy real‑world performance.

*All 10 days contain at least one drive or charge, so the summary truly covers the full period.*
